gate_type: non-standard socket usage required for full bypass — lookup event covers normal TCP/hostname cases
reasoning: The vulnerable code is in a publicly exported function (module.exports default export). No non-default options are required to reach the code path — any hostname-based URL triggers the pre-check bypass at line 30. However, 'gate_breachable' is false because complete exploitation (SSRF filter providing zero protection) requires a non-standard socket scenario where the 'lookup' event is not emitted (Unix domain sockets with options.path instead of options.host, or custom/mocked socket implementations). In the standard/documented usage pattern (HTTP/HTTPS over TCP with axios or node-fetch), the lookup event IS emitted by Node.js net.Socket for all hostname-based connections, so the filter works correctly via the secondary protection layer. The pre-check architectural weakness is real but only becomes an exploitable full bypass in non-standard contexts outside the library's intended scope. The user-controlled parameter (url) is the primary input, and the host is derived from it, so the parameter is user-controlled in typical deployments. Severity stays 'low' as originally assessed.
